claim: Durable Objects provide stateful serverless compute that maintains persistent connections, making them ideal for agent backend infrastructure
actionable: Use Durable Objects instead of stateless functions for AI agent backends that require persistent state and WebSocket connections
source_title: Why Eval++ Is the Next Great Compute Primitive — Sunil Pai & Matt Carey, Cloudflare

claim: Durable Objects achieve sub-20ms latency from London (within single animation frame at 60fps), enabling real-time collaborative features in agent UIs
actionable: Leverage Durable Objects' low latency for real-time collaborative UI features in agent applications without complex distributed systems engineering
source_title: Why Eval++ Is the Next Great Compute Primitive — Sunil Pai & Matt Carey, Cloudflare

claim: Dynamic Workers enable running LLM-generated code in a secure sandboxed isolate with explicit capability grants instead of ambient permissions
actionable: Use Dynamic Workers to safely execute LLM-generated code by explicitly granting only necessary capabilities and blocking unrestricted access
source_title: Why Eval++ Is the Next Great Compute Primitive — Sunil Pai & Matt Carey, Cloudflare

claim: Resumable streaming via Durable Objects automatically enables multi-tab/multi-browser sync without additional distributed systems work
actionable: Build resumable streaming agents on Durable Objects to automatically get multi-device sync capabilities without custom implementation
source_title: Why Eval++ Is the Next Great Compute Primitive — Sunil Pai & Matt Carey, Cloudflare

claim: Durable Objects are particularly well-suited as MCP servers because they maintain stateful connections required by the MCP protocol
actionable: Implement MCP servers using Durable Objects to simplify production deployment of MCP services with built-in state management
source_title: Why Eval++ Is the Next Great Compute Primitive — Sunil Pai & Matt Carey, Cloudflare

claim: Genai code mode allows collapsing 2,600 Cloudflare API endpoints into ~1,000 tokens through LLM-generated code in sandboxed workers
actionable: Use code mode to compress large API surface areas into executable code that agents can dynamically generate and run safely
source_title: Why Eval++ Is the Next Great Compute Primitive — Sunil Pai & Matt Carey, Cloudflare

claim: Generating HTML/React directly instead of JSON for UI enables agents to create more flexible interfaces and avoid JSON generation overhead
actionable: Have agents generate JSX or HTML directly instead of JSON when building dynamic UIs to improve flexibility and reduce intermediate parsing
source_title: Why Eval++ Is the Next Great Compute Primitive — Sunil Pai & Matt Carey, Cloudflare

claim: JavaScript and Python are first-class in Workers; other languages compile to WASM, with Zig providing particularly small bundle sizes
actionable: When writing agent code for Workers, prefer JavaScript/Python for native performance or Zig for compact WASM bundles
source_title: Why Eval++ Is the Next Great Compute Primitive — Sunil Pai & Matt Carey, Cloudflare
